tasks.step: VERIFY: Phase 3 â€” All tool changes complete
tasks.notes: npm run build: clean. npm test: 530 passed, 3 skipped, 34 test files. Mixed-fleet scenario works end-to-end. tool-provider.test.ts: 22 tests covering all 4 providers. No Claude-specific assumptions remain in tool code.

Docs: created docs/provider-matrix.md; updated architecture.md (Provider Abstraction section), tools-lifecycle.md (llm_provider param, update_agent_cli, provider-aware cleanup), tools-work.md (provider table, max_turns note, NDJSON note), tools-infrastructure.md (multi-provider provision_auth, update_agent_cli replaces update_claude), user-guide.md (multi-provider setup, mix-and-match example), vocabulary.md (provider/LLM backend term). Code: apiKeyCheck() parameterized to accept env var name (OsCommands interface, linux.ts, windows.ts, member-detail.ts); 'unrelated Claude processes' string uses provider.name. Security audit: no injection risks â€” escapeDoubleQuoted/escapeGrepPattern/sanitizeSessionId used throughout command building; authEnvVar values are hardcoded constants validated by /^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$/i before use in setEnv/unsetEnv; API keys never logged (only first 10 chars shown in apiKeyCheck); provider.authEnvVar not user-manipulable â€” comes from ProviderAdapter constants.

tasks.step: Add permission config methods to ProviderAdapter
tasks.notes: Added permissionConfigPaths(): string[] and composePermissionConfig(role, allow?): Array<Record|string> to ProviderAdapter interface
Claude: permissionConfigPaths=[.claude/settings.local.json], composePermissionConfig returns [{permissions:{allow}}]
Gemini: 2 paths [.gemini/settings.json, .gemini/policies/fleet.toml], returns [JSON mode obj, TOML string with policy rules + optional tool list]
Codex: path [.codex/config.toml], returns TOML with approval_mode (full-auto/suggest) + sandbox enabled/network flags
Copilot: path [.github/copilot/settings.local.json], returns JSON with allow-all-tools for doer, restrictive allow/deny for reviewer

deliverConfigFile() helper for any path/format; proactive+reactive modes use provider.composePermissionConfig()+permissionConfigPaths(); Claude reactive reads existing JSON; others pass grants to provider; agent.os??'linux' for optional field
Added Provider Permission Mechanisms table with paths/formats for all 4 providers; updated Before every sprint and Mid-sprint denial sections
